17694762.summary: PSH purchased 36,621 shares totalling approximately $1.9m through Jefferies on 17 July 2026. LSE trades (PSH ticker) accounted for 25,462 shares at average 3,888 pence ($52.30), while PSHD trades comprised 11,159 shares at $52.22 average. NAV per share was $77.06. Post-buyback, PSH has 173.8m shares outstanding with 37.2m held in treasury. Shares will be held as treasury stock.
17694762.brief: Pershing Square Holdings repurchased 36,621 shares at average price of $52.28 on 17 July 2026.
17694762.take: Routine share buyback within investment fund's capital management programme; buyback at modest discount to NAV is moderately accretive to remaining shareholders but signals no material strategic shift.

17694763.summary: Neuberger Private Equity Partners Limited repurchased 95,000 Class A Shares on 17 July 2026 at prices ranging from £14.36 to £14.47 per share under shareholder authority granted on 11 June 2026. All bought-back shares will be cancelled, reducing outstanding Class A Shares from 40,829,149 to 40,734,149. The company maintains 3.15m Class A Shares in treasury.
17694763.brief: NBPE completes share buyback of 95,000 Class A shares at £14.36-£14.47, reducing outstanding shares to 40.7m.
17694763.take: Routine capital management action signalling modest shareholder value accretion through buyback; modest scale relative to share base suggests non-event for investors.

17694770.summary: Gulf Keystone Petroleum has temporarily shut-in production at its Shaikan Field in Kurdistan, Iraq as a precautionary measure due to developing regional security conditions, aligning with other international operators in the area. The company's assets remain unimpacted, with latest gross production from Shaikan above 45,000 barrels per day. FY2025 saw average production of 41,560 bopd and adjusted EBITDA of $111.4m. Management will monitor the situation and provide updates as appropriate.
17694770.brief: Gulf Keystone temporarily shuts Shaikan Field production citing regional security concerns; assets unaffected.
17694770.take: Production halt creates near-term cash flow headwind but precautionary nature and intact assets suggest temporary impact; geopolitical risk premium likely priced in by markets already.
